zh.Romney Marsh Liberty, Kent, England: 英國肯特羅姆尼 馬希 利波迪
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Chandler Bate於1270年出生在英國肯特羅姆尼 馬希 利波迪時，他的父親Thomas Dell Bate40歲，母親Mrs Thomas Dell Bate36歲。他和Matilda至少有2個兒子。他去世在他的家鄉。

zh-hans.Romney Marsh Liberty, Kent, England: 英国肯特羅姆尼 馬希 利波迪
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Chandler Bate于1270年出生在英国肯特羅姆尼 馬希 利波迪时，他的父亲Thomas Dell Bate40岁，母亲Mrs Thomas Dell Bate36岁。他和Matilda至少有2个儿子。他去世在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: Bate
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: from the Middle English personal name Bate. It could represent an unrecorded Old English name Bata, or a form of Batte, a short form of Anglo-Norman French names beginning with Bart-, such as Bartelmeu (see Bartholomew ) or Bartram (see Bertram ).
perhaps a topographic name from an unrecorded Middle English antecedent of the modern English dialect bate ‘river island or land liable to flooding’.

variants.name: Bateson
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): patronymic from   Bate   or   Batt  .

variants.name: Batten
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Baten or Batun, a diminutive of Bat (see Bate ).

variants.name: Batey
meanings: English (mainly Northumberland): from a pet form of the Middle English personal name Bate (see Bate ).

variants.name: Batkin
meanings: English (Staffordshire): from the Middle English personal name Batkin, from the personal name Bate + the diminutive suffix -kin.

variants.name: Bartholomew
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Bertilmew, Bertelmy, a borrowing of the Old French form of the Biblical personal name Bartholomew (from Latin Bartholomaeus; Hebrew ‘son of Talmai’, said to mean ‘having many furrows’, i.e. rich in land). This was an extremely popular personal name in Christian Europe, with many vernacular derivatives. It derived its popularity from the apostle Saint Bartholomew (Matthew 10:3), who was, among other things, the patron saint of tanners, vintners, and butlers.
As an Irish name, it has been used as an Anglicized form of Mac Pharthaláin (see McFarlane ).

variants.name: Bateman
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Bateman, Bat(te)man, and in the West Midlands of England Batemon, a pet form of Bate or Batte with the hypocoristic suffix -man.

variants.name: Bates
meanings: English: variant of Bate with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is also common in Ireland.
Americanized form of German   Betz  . See also   Betts  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. nameMeanings.name: Chandler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which originated as an occupational name for someone who made and sold candles (an agent noun from Old French chandele ‘candle’). The extended sense ‘retail dealer’ arose in the 16th century. As a girl's name, it is used mainly in North America.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Chandler Bate was born in 1270, in Romney Marsh Liberty, Kent, England, his father, Thomas Dell Bate, was 40 and his mother, Mrs Thomas Dell Bate, was 36. He had at least 2 sons with Matilda. He died in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Chandler Bate est né en 1270, à Romney Marsh Liberty, Kent, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Dell Bate, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Mrs Thomas Dell Bate, avait 36 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Matilda. Il est décédé dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Chandler Bate 1270 in Romney Marsh Liberty, Kent, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Dell Bate 40 und seine Mutter Mrs Thomas Dell Bate 36. Er hatte mit Matilda mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Chandler Bate è nato il nel 1270 nel Romney Marsh Liberty, Kent, England, il suo padre, Thomas Dell Bate, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Mrs Thomas Dell Bate, aveva 36 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli con Matilda. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.Lydd, Kent, England: イギリスケントリッド
ja.Romney Marsh Liberty, Kent, England: イギリスケントロムニー マーッシュ リバートイ
ja.lifeSummary: Richard Chandler Bateが1270年にイギリスケントロムニー マーッシュ リバートイで生まれた時，父Thomas Dell Bateは40歳，母Mrs Thomas Dell Bateは36歳であった。彼はMatildaとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Chandler Bate(이)가 1270년 영국 켄트 롬니 마시 리버티에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Dell Bate(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs Thomas Dell Bate(은)는 36세였다. 그는 Matilda(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Chandler Bate nasceu em 1270, em Romney Marsh Liberty, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Dell Bate, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Mrs Thomas Dell Bate, tinha 36 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos com Matilda. Ele faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Chandler Bate родился в 1270 г. в местности ромни марш либирти, кэнт, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Dell Bate было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs Thomas Dell Bate, 36. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына от Matilda. Он умер в его родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Chandler Bate nació en 1270, en Romney Marsh Liberty, Kent, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Dell Bate, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Mrs Thomas Dell Bate, tenía 36 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con Matilda. Murió en su pueblo.
